Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Esoteric Buddhism2}} {{buddhism-stub}}

== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Esoteric Buddhism2}} {{buddhism-stub}}

== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Esoteric Buddhism2}} {{buddhism-stub}}

Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Esoteric Buddhism2}} {{buddhism-stub}}

== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shingon Omuro School}}'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shingon Omuro School}}'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shingon Omuro School}}'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds

[[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shingon Omuro School}}'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[Category:Shingon Sect Omuro-ha]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhist sects]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:真言宗御室派]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:hingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]]

[[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shingon Omuro School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro School]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shingon Omuro School}}'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year }}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year }}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Esoteric Buddhism2}} {{buddhism-stub}}

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}'''.

== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year }}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year }}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Esoteric Buddhism2}} {{buddhism-stub}}

== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=draft}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=draft}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|12=draft}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|12=draft}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|12=draft}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=draft}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=draft}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=draft}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|12=draft}} (jointly managed)
