{{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|12=draft|12=draft}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|12=draft|12=draft}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|12=draft|12=draft}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|12=draft|12=draft}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|12=draft|12=draft}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=draft|12=draft}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=draft|12=draft}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=draft|12=draft}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|12=draft|12=draft}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.

revision.comment: 125 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:真言宗御室派 up to rev 98605466
revision.text:
'''真言宗御室派'''（しんごんしゅうおむろは）は、[[日本]]における真言系[[仏教]]宗派のひとつで、[[古義真言宗]]に属する。総本山は'''[[仁和寺]]'''。
== 開祖 == *宗祖: 弘法大師[[空海]] *派祖: 寛平法皇（[[宇多天皇]]）
== 寺格（順不同） == * '''総本山''' 仁和寺（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]） * '''大本山''' [[金剛寺 (河内長野市)|金剛寺]]（[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]）・[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]） * '''準大本山''' [[屋島寺]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） * '''別格本山''' [[龍寶寺 (仙台市)|龍寶寺]]（[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]）、[[蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|蓮華寺]]、[[尊寿院 (京都市)|尊寿院]]（京都府京都市右京区）、[[久米寺]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）、[[神呪寺]]（[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]）、[[本福寺 (淡路市)|本福寺]]（兵庫県[[淡路市]]）、[[福王寺 (広島市)|福王寺]]（広島県[[広島市]]）、[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]（[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]）、[[観龍寺 (倉敷市)|観龍寺]]（岡山県倉敷市）、[[遍照院 (倉敷市)|遍照院]]（岡山県倉敷市）、[[箸蔵寺]]（[[徳島県]][[三好市]]）、[[讃岐国分寺]]（香川県高松市）、[[聖通寺]]（香川県[[宇多津町]]）、[[白峯寺]]（香川県[[坂出市]]）、[[覚城院|不動護国寺]]（香川県[[三豊市]]）、[[出石寺]]（[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]]）、[[海南寺]]（愛媛県[[今治市]]）、[[鎮国寺]]（[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]）、[https://jihoin.jp/ 持宝院]（[[倉敷市|岡山県倉敷市]]） * '''準別格本山''' [[延命寺 (四国中央市)|延命寺]]（愛媛県[[四国中央市]]）、[[大瀧寺]]（徳島県[[美馬市]]）、[[三津寺]]（大阪市中央区）、[[遍照寺 (京都市)|遍照寺]]（京都府京都市右京区） * '''その他の寺院''' [[明通寺]]（[[福井県]][[小浜市]]）、[[慈眼院]]（[[大阪府]][[泉佐野市]]）、 [[初馬寺]]（[[三重県]][[津市]]）、[[林昌寺 (泉南市)|林昌寺]]（大阪府[[泉南市]]）、[[葛井寺]]（[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]）、[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]（[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]）、[[雲辺寺]]（[[徳島県]][[三好市]]）、出釈迦寺（香川県善通寺市）
==沿革== 真言宗御室派の歴史は仁和寺の開創に始まる。[[真言宗]]の事相の流派「[[広沢流]]」の本拠として発展し、仁和寺[[門跡]]に2世[[性信入道親王]]（大御室）が就任されて以降、江戸時代末期まで門跡には[[法親王]]（[[皇族]]）を迎えた。[[1167年]]（[[仁安 (日本)|仁安]]2年）5世[[覚性入道親王]]（紫金台寺御室）が綱所の印璽を下賜され、日本総法務に任ぜられると諸宗格山を支配し、日本仏教界に君臨した。塔頭寺院・子院が60余を数えた時期もあった。なお[[家紋|紋所]]は、桜に引両である。
中世に入ると、[[応仁の乱]]の戦火で、仁和寺が全焼したことが原因で衰退した。戦国期以降は[[足利氏]]・[[織田氏]]・[[豊臣氏]]が復興に努めたが、本格的な再興には至らなかった。
[[江戸時代]]に入り、[[江戸幕府]]による資金援助があり、現在地に堂塔の再建がなり、再興を果たした。
[[明治政府]]の宗教政策により、他の[[真言宗]]宗派と[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）に合同する。[[勅命]]により、30世純仁法親王（楞厳定院御室）が[[還俗]]し改称した[[小松宮彰仁親王]]が、[[1883年]]（明治16年）に仁和会を組織し、仁和寺の復興に尽力。31世以降は門跡に皇族を迎えることは無くなった。
[[1900年]]（明治33年）9月、仁和寺を本山とする真言宗御室派として独立する。
[[1926年]]（[[大正]]15年）には（[[高野山真言宗|真言宗高野派]]（総本山[[金剛峯寺]]）・[[真言宗大覚寺派]]（大本山[[大覚寺]]））と合同して金剛峯寺を総本山とする古義真言宗を組織する。
[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）3月、古義真言宗・新義真言宗系の宗派が政府の政策によって合同し、大真言宗が成立する。が、戦後独立し、[[1946年]]（昭和21年）、再び真言宗御室派として発足した。
== 仁和寺門跡歴代== #[[宇多天皇]]（[[光孝天皇]]第7皇子）：空理・金剛覚 #大御室（[[三条天皇]]第4皇子）：[[性信入道親王]] #中御室（[[白河天皇]]第3皇子）：[[覚行法親王]]（覚念） #高野御室（白河天皇第4皇子）：[[覚法法親王]]（真行） #紫金台寺（しこんだいじ）御室（[[鳥羽天皇]]第5皇子）：[[覚性入道親王]]（信法） #喜多院御室（[[後白河天皇]]第2皇子）：[[守覚法親王]]（守法） #後高野御室（後白河天皇第8皇子）：[[道法法親王]]（尊性） #光台院御室（[[後鳥羽天皇]]第2皇子）：[[道助入道親王]] #金剛定院御室（[[守貞親王|後高倉院]]第2皇子）：[[道深法親王]] #開田准后（[[藤原道家]]第5子）：[[法助]][[准后]] #後中御室（[[後嵯峨天皇]]第6皇子）：[[性助法親王]] #高雄御室（[[後深草天皇]]第4皇子）：[[性仁法親王]] #尊勝院御室（後深草天皇第6皇子）：[[深性法親王]] #常瑜伽院御室（[[伏見天皇]]第3皇子）：[[寛性法親王]] #禅河院御室（[[後伏見天皇]]第3皇子）：[[法守法親王]] #後常瑜伽院御室（[[後光厳天皇]]第5皇子）：[[永助法親王]]（空助） <!--#御室准后（[[源義満]]子）：[[法尊]][[准后]]--> #法金剛院御室（[[後小松天皇]][[猶子]]）：[[承道法親王]] #後光台院御室（[[後花園天皇]]猶子）：[[法深法親王]]（弘覚・静覚） #後禅河院御室（[[後柏原天皇]]第2皇子）：[[覚道法親王]] #厳島御室（[[伏見宮貞敦親王]]第4子）：[[任助法親王]] #後南御室（[[後陽成天皇]]第1皇子）：[[覚深法親王]] #後大御室（[[後水尾天皇]]第3皇子）：[[承法法親王]]（性承） #後金剛定院御室（[[霊元天皇]]第2皇子）：[[覚観法親王]]（覚恕・覚隆） #後光明寿院御室（[[京極宮文仁親王]]第2子）：[[守恕法親王]] #宝荘厳院御室（[[中御門天皇]]第4皇子）：[[慈仁法親王]] #三摩耶心院御室（中御門天皇第6皇子）：[[遵仁法親王]] #金剛心院御室（[[有栖川宮職仁親王]]第3子）：[[覚仁法親王]] #後喜多院御室（[[閑院宮典仁親王]]第2子）：[[深仁法親王]] #不壊身院御室（[[有栖川宮職仁親王]]第11子）：[[済仁法親王]] #楞厳定院御室（[[伏見宮邦家親王]]第5子）：純仁法親王（勅命により復飾、還俗して[[小松宮彰仁親王]]と改称する） #冷泉照道 #冷泉玄誉 #別處栄厳 #[[釈雲照]] #[[泉智等]] #[[土宜法龍]] #浦上隆応 #石堂恵猛 #岡本慈航 #花桝智勝 #[[森諦圓]] #小田慈舟 #立部瑞祐 #小林隆仁 #松村祐澄 #吉田裕信 #堀智範 #佐藤令宜 #南揚道 #立部祐道 #瀬川大秀
== 宗務組織 == *'''管長'''（仁和寺門跡が就任。宗派の代表役員。任期あり。選挙制） **最高顧問・顧問会（管長の諮問機関・非公開） *'''宗務庁''' **宗務総長（仁和寺執行長が就任。責任役員・任期4年） **執行（執行長を置く） **責任役員会（5名） **総務部 **教学部 **財務部 *'''宗会'''（公選議員14名・特選議員3名（計17名）で構成。任期4年。） *地方教区に宗務支所（三重・京都・奈良・和歌山・大阪・兵庫・備前・美作・広島・山口・香川・愛媛・徳島・福岡・肥前）の17ヶ所。宗務支所が設置されていない所は直轄とする。
=== 関連組織 === *仁和会 *仁和寺護持財団
==僧階・僧籍== *'''僧階'''（全15級） **1級 [[大僧正]] **2級 権大僧正 **3級 中僧正 **4級 権中僧正 **5級 少僧正 **6級 権小僧正 **7級 大僧都 **8級 権大僧都 **9級 中僧都 **10級 権中僧都 **11級 少僧都 **12級 権少僧都 **13級 大律師 **14級 律師 **15級 権律師 *権大僧都（真言宗各派経営の大学卒業者と細則の該当者）その他の僧階は細則による。 **真言宗他派から御室派へ転入する僧侶・教師の僧階は、旧所属宗派の僧階をそのまま継承できない。御室派の管長より、僧階の補任を受け、礼録を納めなければならない。 *教階（主教・弘教・示教・司教・補教） *住職（[[得度]]と[[度牒]]を受け、[[四度加行]]の後、[[伝法灌頂]]に入壇。練行を行った者） *名誉住職 *教師（僧侶で教師の補任された者。教師に補任されることが、住職の就任条件でもある） **名誉住職または教師が教師義納金の納付を5年以上未納した場合は僧籍を削除する。 *布教師教階（輔教・司教・示教・弘教・主教） また、（教師の研鑽と補任のために）教学審議会を開催する。
==年中行事== *1月1日: [[修正会]] *1月6日: 御室流華道生初式 *1月7日: 初[[祈祷]] *1月上旬: 御修法遥拝 *1月21日: 献米供 *2月3日: [[大般若転読]]法要（[[節分]]会） *2月15日: [[常楽会]] *2月下旬: [[土砂加持]]法要 *3月21日: 正御影供・[[彼岸]]会 *4月1日～: 霊宝館・春季名宝展開催（50日間） *4月8日: 仏生会 *[[桜]]開花期～: さくら祭り *4月18日: 観音大祭 *5月中旬: 御室流華道全国挿華大会 *5月下旬: 大般若転読法要 *6月15日: 宗祖弘法大師降誕会 *9月8日: 開山忌 *9月21日: 彼岸会 *10月1日～: 霊宝館秋季名宝展開催（50日間） === 毎月行事 === *毎月21日: 御影供 *毎月28日: [[大黒天]]招福[[護摩]]供 ===臨時法会=== *宗祖誕生・入定遠忌法会（50年ごと） *開山寛平法皇[[遠忌]]法会（50年ごと） *歴代法親王・門跡[[追善]]法会（50年ごと） *成就山八十八ヶ所開創法会（50年ごと） *先師忌（年回忌）
== 教育機関 == *[[仁和伝法所]](仁和寺[[所管]]） *[[仁和密教学院]]（[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）設立・仁和寺内）（仁和寺所管） *御室流華道総司庁 *[[種智院大学]]（協同経営） *[[洛南高等学校・附属中学校]]（協同経営）
== 施設 == *霊宝館（仁和寺内に設置・期間限定で有料一般公開される） *御室会館（仁和寺内、[[宿坊]]） *御室老人いこいの家（京都市委託経営）
==教義== [[古義真言宗]]の教義に準じる。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.ninnaji.jp/ 総本山仁和寺] *[http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ 種智院大学公式ホームページ] *[http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ 洛南高等学校・附属中学校]

{{DEFAULTSORT:しんこんしゆうおむろは}} [[Category:真言宗御室派|*]] [[Category:真言宗の宗派|おむろは]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[真言宗御室派]] to [[Shingon Omuro School]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''真言宗御室派'''（しんごんしゅうおむろは）は、[[日本]]における真言系[[仏教]]宗派のひとつで、[[古義真言宗]]に属する。総本山は'''[[仁和寺]]'''。
== 開祖 == *宗祖: 弘法大師[[空海]] *派祖: 寛平法皇（[[宇多天皇]]）
== 寺格（順不同） == * '''総本山''' 仁和寺（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]） * '''大本山''' [[金剛寺 (河内長野市)|金剛寺]]（[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]）・[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]） * '''準大本山''' [[屋島寺]]（[[香川県]][[高松市]]） * '''別格本山''' [[龍寶寺 (仙台市)|龍寶寺]]（[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]）、[[蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|蓮華寺]]、[[尊寿院 (京都市)|尊寿院]]（京都府京都市右京区）、[[久米寺]]（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）、[[神呪寺]]（[[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]）、[[本福寺 (淡路市)|本福寺]]（兵庫県[[淡路市]]）、[[福王寺 (広島市)|福王寺]]（広島県[[広島市]]）、[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]（[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]]）、[[観龍寺 (倉敷市)|観龍寺]]（岡山県倉敷市）、[[遍照院 (倉敷市)|遍照院]]（岡山県倉敷市）、[[箸蔵寺]]（[[徳島県]][[三好市]]）、[[讃岐国分寺]]（香川県高松市）、[[聖通寺]]（香川県[[宇多津町]]）、[[白峯寺]]（香川県[[坂出市]]）、[[覚城院|不動護国寺]]（香川県[[三豊市]]）、[[出石寺]]（[[愛媛県]][[大洲市]]）、[[海南寺]]（愛媛県[[今治市]]）、[[鎮国寺]]（[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]）、[https://jihoin.jp/ 持宝院]（[[倉敷市|岡山県倉敷市]]） * '''準別格本山''' [[延命寺 (四国中央市)|延命寺]]（愛媛県[[四国中央市]]）、[[大瀧寺]]（徳島県[[美馬市]]）、[[三津寺]]（大阪市中央区）、[[遍照寺 (京都市)|遍照寺]]（京都府京都市右京区） * '''その他の寺院''' [[明通寺]]（[[福井県]][[小浜市]]）、[[慈眼院]]（[[大阪府]][[泉佐野市]]）、 [[初馬寺]]（[[三重県]][[津市]]）、[[林昌寺 (泉南市)|林昌寺]]（大阪府[[泉南市]]）、[[葛井寺]]（[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]）、[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]（[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]）、[[雲辺寺]]（[[徳島県]][[三好市]]）、出釈迦寺（香川県善通寺市）
==沿革== 真言宗御室派の歴史は仁和寺の開創に始まる。[[真言宗]]の事相の流派「[[広沢流]]」の本拠として発展し、仁和寺[[門跡]]に2世[[性信入道親王]]（大御室）が就任されて以降、江戸時代末期まで門跡には[[法親王]]（[[皇族]]）を迎えた。[[1167年]]（[[仁安 (日本)|仁安]]2年）5世[[覚性入道親王]]（紫金台寺御室）が綱所の印璽を下賜され、日本総法務に任ぜられると諸宗格山を支配し、日本仏教界に君臨した。塔頭寺院・子院が60余を数えた時期もあった。なお[[家紋|紋所]]は、桜に引両である。
中世に入ると、[[応仁の乱]]の戦火で、仁和寺が全焼したことが原因で衰退した。戦国期以降は[[足利氏]]・[[織田氏]]・[[豊臣氏]]が復興に努めたが、本格的な再興には至らなかった。
[[江戸時代]]に入り、[[江戸幕府]]による資金援助があり、現在地に堂塔の再建がなり、再興を果たした。
[[明治政府]]の宗教政策により、他の[[真言宗]]宗派と[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）に合同する。[[勅命]]により、30世純仁法親王（楞厳定院御室）が[[還俗]]し改称した[[小松宮彰仁親王]]が、[[1883年]]（明治16年）に仁和会を組織し、仁和寺の復興に尽力。31世以降は門跡に皇族を迎えることは無くなった。
[[1900年]]（明治33年）9月、仁和寺を本山とする真言宗御室派として独立する。
[[1926年]]（[[大正]]15年）には（[[高野山真言宗|真言宗高野派]]（総本山[[金剛峯寺]]）・[[真言宗大覚寺派]]（大本山[[大覚寺]]））と合同して金剛峯寺を総本山とする古義真言宗を組織する。
[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）3月、古義真言宗・新義真言宗系の宗派が政府の政策によって合同し、大真言宗が成立する。が、戦後独立し、[[1946年]]（昭和21年）、再び真言宗御室派として発足した。
== 仁和寺門跡歴代== #[[宇多天皇]]（[[光孝天皇]]第7皇子）：空理・金剛覚 #大御室（[[三条天皇]]第4皇子）：[[性信入道親王]] #中御室（[[白河天皇]]第3皇子）：[[覚行法親王]]（覚念） #高野御室（白河天皇第4皇子）：[[覚法法親王]]（真行） #紫金台寺（しこんだいじ）御室（[[鳥羽天皇]]第5皇子）：[[覚性入道親王]]（信法） #喜多院御室（[[後白河天皇]]第2皇子）：[[守覚法親王]]（守法） #後高野御室（後白河天皇第8皇子）：[[道法法親王]]（尊性） #光台院御室（[[後鳥羽天皇]]第2皇子）：[[道助入道親王]] #金剛定院御室（[[守貞親王|後高倉院]]第2皇子）：[[道深法親王]] #開田准后（[[藤原道家]]第5子）：[[法助]][[准后]] #後中御室（[[後嵯峨天皇]]第6皇子）：[[性助法親王]] #高雄御室（[[後深草天皇]]第4皇子）：[[性仁法親王]] #尊勝院御室（後深草天皇第6皇子）：[[深性法親王]] #常瑜伽院御室（[[伏見天皇]]第3皇子）：[[寛性法親王]] #禅河院御室（[[後伏見天皇]]第3皇子）：[[法守法親王]] #後常瑜伽院御室（[[後光厳天皇]]第5皇子）：[[永助法親王]]（空助） <!--#御室准后（[[源義満]]子）：[[法尊]][[准后]]--> #法金剛院御室（[[後小松天皇]][[猶子]]）：[[承道法親王]] #後光台院御室（[[後花園天皇]]猶子）：[[法深法親王]]（弘覚・静覚） #後禅河院御室（[[後柏原天皇]]第2皇子）：[[覚道法親王]] #厳島御室（[[伏見宮貞敦親王]]第4子）：[[任助法親王]] #後南御室（[[後陽成天皇]]第1皇子）：[[覚深法親王]] #後大御室（[[後水尾天皇]]第3皇子）：[[承法法親王]]（性承） #後金剛定院御室（[[霊元天皇]]第2皇子）：[[覚観法親王]]（覚恕・覚隆） #後光明寿院御室（[[京極宮文仁親王]]第2子）：[[守恕法親王]] #宝荘厳院御室（[[中御門天皇]]第4皇子）：[[慈仁法親王]] #三摩耶心院御室（中御門天皇第6皇子）：[[遵仁法親王]] #金剛心院御室（[[有栖川宮職仁親王]]第3子）：[[覚仁法親王]] #後喜多院御室（[[閑院宮典仁親王]]第2子）：[[深仁法親王]] #不壊身院御室（[[有栖川宮職仁親王]]第11子）：[[済仁法親王]] #楞厳定院御室（[[伏見宮邦家親王]]第5子）：純仁法親王（勅命により復飾、還俗して[[小松宮彰仁親王]]と改称する） #冷泉照道 #冷泉玄誉 #別處栄厳 #[[釈雲照]] #[[泉智等]] #[[土宜法龍]] #浦上隆応 #石堂恵猛 #岡本慈航 #花桝智勝 #[[森諦圓]] #小田慈舟 #立部瑞祐 #小林隆仁 #松村祐澄 #吉田裕信 #堀智範 #佐藤令宜 #南揚道 #立部祐道 #瀬川大秀
== 宗務組織 == *'''管長'''（仁和寺門跡が就任。宗派の代表役員。任期あり。選挙制） **最高顧問・顧問会（管長の諮問機関・非公開） *'''宗務庁''' **宗務総長（仁和寺執行長が就任。責任役員・任期4年） **執行（執行長を置く） **責任役員会（5名） **総務部 **教学部 **財務部 *'''宗会'''（公選議員14名・特選議員3名（計17名）で構成。任期4年。） *地方教区に宗務支所（三重・京都・奈良・和歌山・大阪・兵庫・備前・美作・広島・山口・香川・愛媛・徳島・福岡・肥前）の17ヶ所。宗務支所が設置されていない所は直轄とする。
=== 関連組織 === *仁和会 *仁和寺護持財団
==僧階・僧籍== *'''僧階'''（全15級） **1級 [[大僧正]] **2級 権大僧正 **3級 中僧正 **4級 権中僧正 **5級 少僧正 **6級 権小僧正 **7級 大僧都 **8級 権大僧都 **9級 中僧都 **10級 権中僧都 **11級 少僧都 **12級 権少僧都 **13級 大律師 **14級 律師 **15級 権律師 *権大僧都（真言宗各派経営の大学卒業者と細則の該当者）その他の僧階は細則による。 **真言宗他派から御室派へ転入する僧侶・教師の僧階は、旧所属宗派の僧階をそのまま継承できない。御室派の管長より、僧階の補任を受け、礼録を納めなければならない。 *教階（主教・弘教・示教・司教・補教） *住職（[[得度]]と[[度牒]]を受け、[[四度加行]]の後、[[伝法灌頂]]に入壇。練行を行った者） *名誉住職 *教師（僧侶で教師の補任された者。教師に補任されることが、住職の就任条件でもある） **名誉住職または教師が教師義納金の納付を5年以上未納した場合は僧籍を削除する。 *布教師教階（輔教・司教・示教・弘教・主教） また、（教師の研鑽と補任のために）教学審議会を開催する。
==年中行事== *1月1日: [[修正会]] *1月6日: 御室流華道生初式 *1月7日: 初[[祈祷]] *1月上旬: 御修法遥拝 *1月21日: 献米供 *2月3日: [[大般若転読]]法要（[[節分]]会） *2月15日: [[常楽会]] *2月下旬: [[土砂加持]]法要 *3月21日: 正御影供・[[彼岸]]会 *4月1日～: 霊宝館・春季名宝展開催（50日間） *4月8日: 仏生会 *[[桜]]開花期～: さくら祭り *4月18日: 観音大祭 *5月中旬: 御室流華道全国挿華大会 *5月下旬: 大般若転読法要 *6月15日: 宗祖弘法大師降誕会 *9月8日: 開山忌 *9月21日: 彼岸会 *10月1日～: 霊宝館秋季名宝展開催（50日間） === 毎月行事 === *毎月21日: 御影供 *毎月28日: [[大黒天]]招福[[護摩]]供 ===臨時法会=== *宗祖誕生・入定遠忌法会（50年ごと） *開山寛平法皇[[遠忌]]法会（50年ごと） *歴代法親王・門跡[[追善]]法会（50年ごと） *成就山八十八ヶ所開創法会（50年ごと） *先師忌（年回忌）
== 教育機関 == *[[仁和伝法所]](仁和寺[[所管]]） *[[仁和密教学院]]（[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）設立・仁和寺内）（仁和寺所管） *御室流華道総司庁 *[[種智院大学]]（協同経営） *[[洛南高等学校・附属中学校]]（協同経営）
== 施設 == *霊宝館（仁和寺内に設置・期間限定で有料一般公開される） *御室会館（仁和寺内、[[宿坊]]） *御室老人いこいの家（京都市委託経営）
==教義== [[古義真言宗]]の教義に準じる。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.ninnaji.jp/ 総本山仁和寺] *[http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ 種智院大学公式ホームページ] *[http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ 洛南高等学校・附属中学校]

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds

[[sn:Shingon Omuro School]] [[ja:真言宗御室派]] [[sn:hingon Omuro School]] [[en:Shingon Omuro School]]

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1900|qq=}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchiin University|qq=}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo-ku|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachinagano|qq=}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishō-in (Miyajima)|qq=}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsukaichi|qq=}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu|qq=}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku|qq=}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara|qq=}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnō-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya|qq=}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikokuchuo|qq=}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima|qq=}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōtsū-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukui Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama|qq=}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu|qq=}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi clan|qq=}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1900|qq=}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchiin University|qq=}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchiin University|qq=}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo-ku|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachinagano|qq=}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishō-in (Miyajima)|qq=}} ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsukaichi|qq=}}) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': {{ill|Yashima-ji|ja|屋島寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Takamatsu|ja|高松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu|qq=}}) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku|qq=}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara|qq=}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnō-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya|qq=}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikokuchuo|qq=}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima|qq=}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōtsū-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukui Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama|qq=}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu|qq=}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi clan|qq=}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1900|qq=}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchiin University|qq=}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[sn:Shingon Omuro School]] [[ja:真言宗御室派]] [[sn:hingon Omuro School]] [[en:Shingon Omuro School]]

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''{{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ({{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}})
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}} {{ill|Ukyo-ku|ja|右京区|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo-ku|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qq=}}) * '''Major Head Temples''': {{ill|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|ja|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|qq=}} {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachinagano|qq=}}), {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院 (宮島)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishō-in (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}} {{ill|Aoba-ku|ja|青葉区 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku|qq=}}), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara|qq=}}), {{ill|Kōnō-ji|ja|神呪寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnō-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya|qq=}}), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Shikokuchuo|ja|四国中央市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikokuchuo|qq=}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Mima|ja|美馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima|qq=}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|ja|明通寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōtsū-ji|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukui Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Obama|ja|小浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obama|qq=}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tsu|ja|津市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsu|qq=}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|ja|応仁の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnin War|qq=}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Oda clan|ja|織田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oda clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|ja|豊臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi clan|qq=}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1900|qq=}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|ja|種智院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchiin University|qq=}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

revision.text:
'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1900|qq=}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

'''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|ja|明治12年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1879年}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|ja|明治33年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1900|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1900年}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
