{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q420739]] Wikidata name: Omuro school [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|qq=|1=Renge-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|qq=|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|qq=|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|qq=|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=|1=Jigen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|qq=|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|qq=|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|qq=|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|qq=|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q420739]] Wikidata name: Omuro school [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|1=Renge-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds

[[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|1=Renge-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q420739]] Wikidata name: Omuro school [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|1=Renge-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.

== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)

based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[Category:Schools of Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Shingon Omuro branch]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:真言宗御室派]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q420739}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q420739]] Wikidata name: Omuro school [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|1=Renge-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗御室派]][[zh:真言宗御室派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q420739]] Wikidata name: Omuro school [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro-ha of the Shingon Sect''' (真言宗御室派) is one of the Shingon Buddhist sects in Japan, belonging to [[古義真言宗|Kogi Shingon Buddhism]]. Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|1=Renge-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Its headquarters is located at the '''[[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]'''.
== Founders == * Sect Founder: Kōbō Daishi ([[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]]) * Sub-sect Founder: Emperor Kanpyō ([[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]])
== Temple Ranking (unordered) == * '''Head Temple''': [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] ([[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] [[:en:Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto]]) * '''Major Head Temples''': [[:en:Kongō-ji|Kongō-ji (Kawachinagano)]] ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] [[:en:Kawachinagano|Kawachinagano]]), [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in (Miyajima)]] ([[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]]) * '''Semi-major Head Temples''': [[:en:Yashima-ji|Yashima-ji]] ([[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu]]) * '''Special Head Temples''': {{ill|Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja|龍寶寺 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūhō-ji (Sendai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] [[:en:Sendai|Sendai]] [[:en:Aoba-ku, Sendai|Aoba-ku]]), {{ill|Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|ja|蓮華寺 (京都市右京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renge-ji (Ukyo-ku)|1=Renge-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621738}}, {{ill|Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja|尊寿院 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonju-in (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kume-dera|ja|久米寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume-dera|1=Kume-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369593}} ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]]), [[:en:Kannō-ji|Kōnō-ji]] ([[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya]]), and others. * '''Semi-special Head Temples''': {{ill|Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|ja|延命寺 (四国中央市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmei-ji (Shikokuchuo)|1=Enmei-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486492}} ([[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]] [[:en:Shikokuchūō|Shikokuchuo]]), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ([[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima]]), and others. * '''Other Temples''': [[:en:Myōtsū-ji|Myōtsū-ji]] ([[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]] [[:en:Obama, Fukui|Obama]]), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ([[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] [[:en:Tsu, Mie|Tsu]]), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]]. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]]. Efforts to rebuild by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], [[:en:Oda clan|Oda clan]], and [[:en:Toyotomi clan|Toyotomi clan]] were unsuccessful.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In [[:en:1879|Meiji 12]], Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in [[:en:1900|Meiji 33]], Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{Vajrayana}} {{buddhism-stub}}

Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga clan}}, {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=Meiji 12}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|en|1900|WD=Q2034|lt=Meiji 33}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
