Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}} {{ill|Shikokuchūō|en|Shikokuchūō|WD=Q838666|lt=Shikokuchuo}}), {{ill|Ōtaki-ji|ja|大瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki-ji|1=Otaki-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11437672}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|en|Mima, Tokushima|WD=Q857828|lt=Mima}}), and others. * '''Other Temples''': {{ill|Myōtsū-ji|en|Myōtsū-ji|WD=Q3331935|lt=Myōtsū-ji}} ({{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}} {{ill|Obama, Fukui|en|Obama, Fukui|WD=Q41773|lt=Obama}}), {{ill|Jigen-in|ja|慈眼院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigen-in|1=Jigen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495035}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|WD=Q852018|lt=Izumisano}}), {{ill|Hatsuma-dera|ja|初馬寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuma-dera|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蓮光院初馬寺|1=Renkō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621643}} ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|WD=Q203027|lt=Tsu}}), and others.
== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga clan}}, {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=Meiji 12}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|en|1900|WD=Q2034|lt=Meiji 33}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|en|Shuchiin University|WD=Q7504609|lt=Shuchiin University}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
== External Links == * [http://www.ninnaji.jp/ Official Ninna-ji Website] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University Official Website] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga clan}}, {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=Meiji 12}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|en|1900|WD=Q2034|lt=Meiji 33}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Shusho-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.

== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.

== History == The history of Omuro-ha began with the foundation of {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}}. Developed as the base of the {{ill|Hirozawa School|ja|広沢流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirozawa School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 真言宗|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, Ninna-ji served as a headquarters for Shingon Buddhism. Since the appointment of the second head, {{ill|Prince Shōshin|ja|性信入道親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōshin|1=Shōshin-nyūdōshinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491592}}, Ninna-ji received Imperial patronage and developed significantly, counting over 60 branch temples at its peak.
During the Middle Ages, the sect declined due to the destruction of Ninna-ji during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}. Efforts to rebuild by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga clan|1=Ashikaga clan}}, {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|WD=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}}, and {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|WD=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan|1=Toyotomi clan}} were unsuccessful.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, funding from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} allowed for the reconstruction of Ninna-ji's halls and towers.
In {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=Meiji 12|1=1879}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|en|1900|WD=Q2034|lt=Meiji 33|1=1900}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Q11386339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|en|Shuchiin University|WD=Q7504609|lt=Shuchiin University|1=Shuchiin University}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds

In {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=Meiji 12|1=1879}}, Omuro-ha merged with other Shingon sects. Later, in {{ill|1900|en|1900|WD=Q2034|lt=Meiji 33|1=1900}}, Omuro-ha declared independence with Ninna-ji as its head temple.
== Leadership Structure == * '''Chief Abbot''' (elected, represents the sect) * '''Religious Administration Office''' (consisting of departments like General Affairs, Education, and Finance) * '''Assembly''' (17 members: 14 elected, 3 appointed)
== Doctrine == Omuro-ha follows the teachings of {{ill|Kogi Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogi Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}}.
== Annual Events == * January 1: {{ill|Shusho-e|ja|修正会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusho-e|1=Q11386339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386339}} * February 3: {{ill|Daihannya Tendoku|ja|大般若転読|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya Tendoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Ceremony * March 21: {{ill|Spring Higan|ja|彼岸会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Higan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彼岸|1=Higan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q866977}} Ceremony * June 15: Celebration of Kōbō Daishi’s birth * Other notable events include seasonal festivals and memorial services.
== Educational Institutions == * {{ill|Ninna Denbōjo|ja|仁和伝法所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Denbōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja|仁和密教学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna Esoteric Academy|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shuchiin University|en|Shuchiin University|WD=Q7504609|lt=Shuchiin University|1=Shuchiin University}} (jointly managed)
== Facilities == * {{ill|Reihōkan|ja|霊宝館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reihōkan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Exhibits treasures of Ninna-ji) * Lodging facilities within Ninna-ji grounds
